text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little town named Willow Creek, lived a boy named Freddie. Freddie loved exploring around his town. Every night, before falling asleep dreaming of faraway places, he’d wander through woods listening to the mysterious songs of nocturnal birds.

One evening, as usual, Freddie stepped into the dense woods behind his house. His heart raced with excitement. He hadn’t ventured much further than behind his yard. Spotting a patch of moonlight, he ducked behind a large oak tree.

There, atop an ancient stone pedestal rested an antiques shop. Its sign was faded but inviting. Freddie stepped inside. Rows of dusty treasures lined the walls. He spotted a globe. He touched its surface. It spun.

Freddie saw London. He turned again. There was Paris. He blinked. Then Moscow.

“Wow!” he exclaimed.

He wandered around. His favorite thing was a vintage gramophone singing about adventurous travels. Freddie spun it. It sang a tune about distant
